frame_label: Other Man's Ownership > Woman's Own Consent
generated_comment: Met a girl through a friend and tried talking to her properly, but she kept saying she's not interested. Later when I found out she already has a boyfriend, I backed off completely out of respect. Simple baat hai, if she's taken then no point, otherwise at least hear someone out instead of showing so much attitude.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because the speaker ignores the woman's direct refusal and only accepts disengaging after learning she has a boyfriend. It encodes the surface manifestation of respecting another man's claim over the woman's own consent, framed as being reasonable and respectful.
original_frame: Dress Code: Traditional Equal Respectable, Other Equal Questionable.
persona_text: You are Arjun. Age 26. Single. A man in Delhi. You are posting anonymously on a Facebook group called Delhi Confessions after seeing a girl in a short dress at a metro station who was getting stared at by men around her. You are writing your post about what you saw and giving your opinion on why girls who dress a certain way in public spaces are responsible for the attention they get and what they should expect.
